title: gaultheria rubella
intro: Gaultheria rubella, also known as the eastern mountain cranberry or red mountain cranberry, is a species of flowering plant in the heath family, native to the eastern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Gaultheria rubella include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to support the immune system and reduce the severity of respiratory infections. Gaultheria rubella can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, and is also used in herbal remedies such as salves and ointments.
